title: Agroha Dham
tagline: Agroha Dham — spiritual town of Haryana

overview.short: Agroha is a Hindu temple complex in Agroha city of Hisar District of Haryana state of India.
overview.description: Agroha is a Hindu temple complex in Agroha city of Hisar District of Haryana state of India. Construction started in 1976 and was completed in 1984. The main temple is dedicated to the Hindu goddess Mahalakshmi. The temple complex is located adjacent to the Agroha Mound and near Maharaja Agrasen Medical College.

topPlaces.name: Frain Kalan
topPlaces.description: Frain Kalan is a village in the mandal of Narwana, in Jind district in the Indian state of Haryana.

items.note: Frain Kalan is a village in the mandal of Narwana, in Jind district in the Indian state of Haryana.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Agroha Dham?
faq.a: The best time to visit Agroha Dham is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Agroha Dham?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (146 km) and the nearest railway station is Ambala Cantt Junction (121 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Agroha Dham?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Agroha Dham at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Agroha Dham?
faq.a: Stays in Agroha Dham start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Agroha Dham?
faq.a: The top attractions are Frain Kalan.
